Backrunning is a practice where a malicious actor monitors the mempool for pending transactions and then executes their own transaction immediately after the target transaction to exploit price discrepancies-manufacturer
This technique often involves using smart contracts to automate the detection and execution of these trades-manufacturer
Code examples typically show how to listen for specific events on the blockchain and then submit a transaction with a higher gas fee to ensure priority-manufacturer
Such actions can negatively impact legitimate users by draining liquidity or altering expected outcomes-manufacturer
Developers must implement protections like commit-reveal schemes or time-locked transactions to mitigate these risks-manufacturer
Understanding the mechanics of backrunning is crucial for creating secure and fair decentralized applications-manufacturer
